narrative:
Our offense is positioned for a high-ceiling performance today, headlined by Christian Yelich and Yordan Alvarez, who boast the day's top projected scores of 87.88 and 84.75, respectively. Yelich benefits from a favorable matchup against Brayan Bello and a boost from facing a slow catcher, while Alvarez exploits strong park factors and SP skill metrics against Ryan Feltner. Furthermore, Kyle Schwarber remains a premium start; his elite xwOBA metrics combined with a solid BvP track record against Adrian Houser make him a cornerstone for power production in today’s Roto efficiency hunt.

We are exercising caution with the bottom of the lineup, particularly Hyeseong Kim and Kazuma Okamoto, both of whom face significant SP skill headwinds that dampen their overall utility. While Chase Meidroth offers reliable floor value out of the leadoff spot, we must monitor the wind conditions closely, as his "Wind Out" bonus is a key driver of his high projection. Additionally, note that Trea Turner faces a slight efficiency dip today due to an elite-rated catcher behind the plate; while he remains a must-start, his stolen base probability is suppressed by the defensive matchup, requiring us to lean on his contact profile to maintain our daily scoring trajectory.
